EXAM: ULTRASOUND THYROID
HISTORY: Thyroid nodule
TECHNIQUE: Standard grayscale images were acquired with additional Doppler interrogation when
appropriate.
COMPARISON: None available.
FINDINGS:
Right Thyroid Lobe: The right lobe of the thyroid measures 4.7 X 2.8 X 3 cm. The right lobe of the thyroid has a
volume of 18 ml. The right lobe of the thyroid has a normal size. The echotexture of the right lobe appears
normal.
In the left lobe of the thyroid there is a 4.1 cm tall by 2.3 em AP by 3 cm wide, spongiform. isoechoic, taller
than wide nodule with ill-defined margins and no echogenic foci. This TR 4 nodule requires fine-needle
aspiration according to the TI rads protocol.
Left Thyroid Lobe: The left lobe of the thyroid measures 4.7 x 1.1 X 1.4 cm. The left lobe of the thyroid has a
volume of 3 ml. The left lobe of the thyroid is small. The echotexture of the left lobe appears normal.
There are no suspicious nodules in the left lobe of the thyroid gland.
Isthmus: The isthmus appears normal. It measures 2 mm AP.
Pathologic Cervical Lymphadenopathy: None.
The normal thyroid volume for an adult female ranges from 10 to 15 mL.
IMPRESSION: The 4.1 em TR 4 nodule in the right lobe of the thyroid requires fine-needle aspiration
according to the TI rads protocol.
End of diagnostic report for accession:

Non-Gyn Cytology Report
Diagnosis
Thyroid, right, fine-needle aspiration biopsy:
Malignant. consistent with papillary thyroid carcinoma (Bethesda category VI), see
comment
Comment:
The smears prepared from the aspiration cytology are cellular and composed of sheets of
follicular cells with nuclear irregularities. nuclear grooves and occasional pseudoinclusions.
There are also groups of enlarged cells with eccentric nuclei and prominent nucleoli likely
representing Hurthle cell change. Scant material is present in the cellblock and limited
battery of immunohistochemical stains are obtained to exclude a possibility of medullary
carcinoma. There is no immunohistochemical support for medullary carcinoma.
Hemlata N Oswal. MD
(Electronically signed)
Verified: 12/12/2025 12:34
HNO/HNO

Right Thyroid Nodule Tissue

Procedure: Right Thyroid Nodule Biopsy
Post-op diagnosis: Nontoxic single thyroid nodule
Clinical History: See H&P
Gross Description
Received labeled with the patient's name and "right thyroid nodule tissue" are 5 alcohol-fixed
slides for Pap staining, 5 air fixed slides for Wright-Giemsa and 20 ce of 95% alcohol
intermixed with easin. The slides and alcohol/eosin with aspirate are forwarded to PML for
further processing.

CC Physician:
EXAM: US FNA W Guidance Any Area 1st lesion
EXAM DATE:
INDICATION: image guided FNA R lobe 4.1 cm thyroid bx/R thyroid nodule
COMPARISON: 9/12/2025
OPERATOR: Lyudmila Babchanik, PA-C
SUPERVISING PHYSICIAN: Dr. Bhutani
PROCEDURE:
Patient is a 21-year-old female presenting for thyroid biopsy. Patient's previous ultrasound dated
9/12/2025 demonstrated a 4.1 cm TIRADS 4 nodule in the right thyroid that meets criteria for biopsy.
Informed consent was obtained and the risks and benefits of the procedure were explained in detail.
The patient was positioned supine on the bed. The neck was cleaned and draped in the usual sterile
fashion. The right lobe of the thyroid was interrogated with ultrasound demonstrating a 3.6 x 4.2 cm
nodule. 1% lidocaine was used to anesthetize the skin and soft tissues at the skin entry site.
under continuous ultrasound guidance. a 25G needle was advanced into the right thyroid nodule and the
sample was given to the pathologist. This was repeated 4 additional times. The final needle was
removed and hemostasis was achieved. A sterile bandage was placed.
Post biopsy ultrasound images were obtained demonstrating no significant hematoma.
The patient tolerated the procedure well and there were no immediate complications.
IMPRESSION:
Completed US-guided right thyroid nodule fine needle aspiration.
***FINAL***
Dictated by: Babchanik, Lyudmila I PA, PA
Electronically signed by: Babchanik, Lyudmila I
Transcribed by:SP & D: 12/11/2025 12:02, T: 12/11/2025 12:05, S: 12/11/2025 12:06

claude_vision.referral_reason: Surgical consultation for confirmed papillary thyroid carcinoma (malignant neoplasm of thyroid gland, ICD-10 C73) discovered via FNA biopsy of a right thyroid nodule. Referred to ENT/Otolaryngology for surgical management evaluation.
relevant_clinical_findings: 21-year-old female with confirmed papillary thyroid carcinoma (Bethesda Category VI) by FNA biopsy of right thyroid nodule

Pathology (Mercy San Juan Medical Center, signed 12/12/2025): Malignant, consistent with papillary thyroid carcinoma — follicular cells with nuclear irregularities, nuclear grooves, and occasional pseudoinclusions; Hurthle cell change noted
Immunohistochemistry: TTF-1 Positive, Thyroglobulin Positive, Calcitonin Negative, Synaptophysin Negative — supports papillary thyroid carcinoma, excludes medullary carcinoma

Ultrasound (DRI Elk Grove, 9/15/2025): 4.1 cm TR4 nodule in right lobe of thyroid — spongiform, isoechoic, taller than wide, ill-defined margins, no echogenic foci; recommended FNA per TI-RADS protocol
FNA procedure (Mercy San Juan, 12/11/2025): US-guided right thyroid nodule FNA performed on 3.6 x 4.2 cm nodule; 4 additional passes completed; no immediate complications
Physical exam finding: Right thyroid mass noted on neck examination
PCP (Dr. Mikhail Palatnik, Internal Medicine) referred to both ENT/Otolaryngology and Endocrinology/Diabetes & Metabolism for surgical and endocrine management
Patient is not on any medications; no known drug allergies; non-smoker; denies alcohol and recreational drug use
Vitals within normal limits: BP 110/70, HR 80, Temp 98.0F, O2 Sat 98%, BMI 24.62
claude_vision.imaging_summary: Thyroid Ultrasound (DRI Elk Grove, RadNet Imaging Center, date of service ~9/15/2025, signed 09-15-2025 by David Martin, MD): Right lobe measures 4.7 x 2.8 x 3 cm (volume 18 ml, normal size). A 4.1 cm tall x 2.3 cm AP x 3 cm wide spongiform, isoechoic nodule with ill-defined margins and no echogenic foci identified in the right lobe — classified as TR4, requiring FNA per TI-RADS protocol. Left lobe is small (4.7 x 1.1 x 1.4 cm, volume 3 ml) with no suspicious nodules. No pathologic cervical lymphadenopathy identified. Isthmus normal at 2 mm AP. Report IS attached (page 12 of 13 fax).
missing_information: No pre-operative staging imaging (CT neck/chest) included — commonly expected for confirmed thyroid malignancy prior to ENT surgical consultation
No thyroid function labs (TSH, Free T4, Free T3) included
No prior thyroid ultrasound from 9/12/2025 referenced in the FNA procedure report (comparison date mentioned as 9/12/2025) — that earlier ultrasound report is not attached
No endocrinology consultation notes included (referral was also placed to Endocrinology per PCP visit note)
Insurance card (both sides) not included — Sacramento ENT referral form requests inclusion of insurance card
Reason for consultation field on Sacramento ENT referral form (page 08 of 13) is left blank
SacENT MD preference field on referral form is blank — no specific ENT surgeon requested
Number of pages field on Sacramento ENT referral cover sheet is blank

provider_urgency_label.label: urgent
provider_urgency_label.source: Multiple sources: (1) Authorization document (page 03 of 13): 'Priority Status: 1 - URGENT'; (2) Referral Information section (page 2 of referral notes): 'Priority: Urgent'; (3) Health And Life Organization fax cover sheet (page 01 of 13): 'Urgent' checkbox is checked; (4) Scheduling notes (page 1): subject line references 'urg ref expo' (urgent referral expedite); (5) Authorization fax cover sheet (page 02 of 13): FAX number in subject line '20260113710185200005' corresponds to the urgent authorization

ICD-10 diagnosis C73: 'Malignant neoplasm of thyroid gland' listed on authorization (page 03), referral information (page 2 of referral notes), and PCP visit note (page 06 of 13)
PCP visit note chief complaint (page 05 of 13): '*** PAPILLARY THYROID CA BY BX OF R THYROID NODULE'
Consultation request form (page 08 of 13): 'Diagnoses / ICD-9/10: C73 Malignant neoplasm of thyroid gland'
criteria_check.provider_label: Urgent — marked by referring provider in multiple locations including insurance authorization priority status (1 - URGENT), fax cover sheet urgent checkbox, and referral priority field
criteria_check.reasoning: This referral is FLAGGED FOR PRIORITY REVIEW because the clinical content confirms a malignant diagnosis (papillary thyroid carcinoma, Bethesda Category VI) which directly matches the 'confirmed cancer/malignancy' urgent criterion, and the provider's urgent label is fully consistent with and supported by the clinical findings.

claude_vision.summary: This is an urgent ENT/Otolaryngology referral for a 21-year-old Hispanic female with biopsy-confirmed papillary thyroid carcinoma (Bethesda Category VI) of a 4.1 cm right thyroid nodule, referred by PCP Dr. Mikhail Palatnik (Sacramento Community Clinics) for surgical evaluation. The diagnosis was established by ultrasound-guided FNA at Mercy San Juan Medical Center (December 2025), with immunohistochemistry confirming papillary thyroid carcinoma and excluding medullary carcinoma. The referral is marked urgent by the referring provider across multiple documents and meets ENT priority triage criteria for confirmed malignancy; however, pre-operative staging imaging (CT neck/chest) and thyroid function labs are notably absent from the submitted records.
